summary: Tight fire‑filled set with stellar Deep Elem and Wheel jams
blurb: The show bursts open with a scorching New Minglewood Blues and rides a wave of tight playing through Deep Elem, Althea > Looks Like Rain > Deal, and a monster Playing In The Band jam. Jerry’s guitar is on fire, Brent’s Moog textures shimmer, and the drums/space/Wheel segment shines.
review:
December 27, 1981 at Oakland Auditorium Arena captures the Dead in peak ’81 form – a high‑energy marathon that feels both polished and spontaneous. The first set launches with a blistering New Minglewood Blues and rolls through Sugaree, Deep Elem Blues, and a gorgeous Althea → Looks Like Rain → Deal triptych that reviewers call “ripping” and “ecstatic.”  The second set is a showcase for the band’s improvisational muscle: Samson & Delilah leads into To Lay Me Down, then a double‑Playin’ In The Band sandwich bookended by a tight Drums‑Space‑Wheel sequence.  Bill Kreutzmann’s jazzy mid‑show solo on Playing In The Band draws nods to his ’73‑’74 style, while Mickey Hart and the rhythm section lock in for pure magic.  Brent Mydland’s Moog textures and synth flourishes are highlighted throughout, adding a modern sheen to the classic material.  Across all six soundboard recordings the mix is crystal clear, making this one of the most listenable late‑’80 shows – the recommended Gans SBD capture is a “polished gem” that showcases every nuance.

Fans will find the Deep Elem Blues and The Wheel moments especially rewarding, and the encore’s gentle “It’s All Over Now Baby Blue” provides a sweet close to an unforgettable night.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Four out of five analyses are overwhelmingly positive, praising the tightness, fire‑filled playing, standout jams (Deep Elem, Wheel, Playing In The Band) and consistent high energy. Only one negative note concerns a faulty upload, not performance. The consensus yields a strong positive sentiment adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Excellent soundboard source with polished mix, captures the tight fire‑filled performance and all key jams clearly.

key_highlights: Monster Playing In The Band jam that carries the second set
Ripping Althea → Looks Like Rain → Deal closing triptych in the first set
Ecstatic Wheel segment and tight drums/space/drums section
Brent’s Moog textures add a shimmering layer throughout

band_performance.Jerry: Fiery, fluid guitar work that many reviewers call his finest ever, especially on Deep Elem and the Playing In The Band jam.
band_performance.Bob: His spoken ad‑lib at the start of Playing In The Band is described as supremely entertaining and one‑of‑a‑kind.
band_performance.Brent: Moog synth textures praised across the set, adding depth and sparkle to the band’s sound.
band_performance.Mickey: Tight, powerful drumming that drives the drums/space/Wheel segment to ‘pure magic.’
band_performance.Billy: Delivers a jazzy mid‑show solo on Playing In The Band reminiscent of his early‑70s style; overall tight and dynamic percussion.
